summary: Full e2e validation of new super-admin feature set: admin seeding, role-based access, commission tracking, suspend/reactivate, refund, payouts, settings. 18 new admin pytest tests added + all 54 pre-existing tests still pass (72/72 total). Frontend admin dashboard, settings, users, payments, payouts, and landlord Gross/Fee/Net columns all render correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: admin_router.update_settings response calls get_settings(_) passing the admin dict where the dependency expects to inject; works because FastAPI dependency is bypassed when called directly, but it's a fragile pattern. Consider refactoring to a private helper returning the settings dict.

models.Payment Pydantic model does not declare commission_amount / net_to_landlord / commission_rate fields. Mongo stores them and admin endpoints return them, but the typed Payment model is out-of-sync with reality. Same for PaymentStatus 'refunded' is in Literal but Payment model used for response_model in other endpoints would now strip the new fields if you ever switched to response_model=Payment.

payments_router._process_callback_payload uses local var name 'status' which shadows the FastAPI status module (no actual bug because that module isn't imported here, but the variable name is confusing).
compute_commission is imported lazily inside _process_callback_payload to avoid circular import - acceptable, but a small note for future maintainers.

Refunds do not credit a tenant or attempt actual M-Pesa reversal - it's purely a bookkeeping refund (status flipped + bill rolled back / viewing cancelled). This matches the stated design but should be documented clearly in admin UI.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (admin nav + dashboard + settings persistence + landlord Gross/Fee/Net + admin redirect)

test_credentials: admin@nyumbaos.co.ke / admin123 (admin), land@demo.com / demo123 (landlord). Confirmed working.
seed_data_creation: Test creates a temporary landlord account per run (test_suspend_<random>@x.com) to verify suspend->login-block->reactivate->login-success flow. Also books one viewing per run (KES 200) which becomes a succeeded payment with commission_amount=7.00, then refunds it. No persistent pollution since the refund cancels the viewing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All admin endpoints under /api/admin/* require role=admin (require_role('admin'))). Suspend uses /api/admin/users/{id}/suspend with body {suspended: true|false}. Commission only applied to payments succeeding AFTER feature deploy - legacy payments correctly show commission=0 (visible in landlord Payments UI as -KES 0 fee, KES 200 net). Tests confirmed: admin login -> /admin (not /dashboard), sidebar has Platform Overview/Users/All Payments/Payouts/Settings, dashboard tiles render COMMISSION EARNED/GROSS PROCESSED/OWED TO LANDLORDS/TOTAL USERS plus Revenue by Source breakdown. Settings persist after reload. Landlord Payments page now has GROSS/FEE/NET TO YOU columns.
rca of the issue: N/A - no issues found. Feature works end-to-end.
